final_answer:
Phase 1 is implemented.

Why it matters: frontend branding text/labels are now moved off `BlockScout` while keeping the app buildable before deeper namespace/path/infrastructure cutovers.

Fact (confirmed):
- I re-read `AGENTS.md` first, then implemented a frontend content-only pass (no file/path renames).
- `Blockscout|BlockScout` in frontend content is now `0` files (was 100 lines before).
- `52` frontend source/config files were updated (examples: [meta.ts](/srv/repos/gold-chain/scan/blockscout-frontend/configs/app/meta.ts), [description.ts](/srv/repos/gold-chain/scan/blockscout-frontend/lib/metadata/templates/description.ts), [Footer.tsx](/srv/repos/gold-chain/scan/blockscout-frontend/ui/snippets/footer/Footer.tsx), [GasTrackerFaq.tsx](/srv/repos/gold-chain/scan/blockscout-frontend/ui/gasTracker/GasTrackerFaq.tsx)).
- Compile checks passed:
  - `pnpm lint:tsc` passed
  - `pnpm build` passed
- Remaining lowercase matches are `237` files, mostly external/dependency/infrastructure tokens (`@blockscout/*` package names, `*.blockscout.com` URLs, `github.com/blockscout`, `blockscout-frontend` image/tag names), which are not path-renamed in Phase 1 by design.

Current blocker:
- Full zero-match frontend removal is blocked on dependency/infra namespace cutover decisions (package namespace, hostnames, and deployment chart references).

What should be done next:
1. Phase 1b: replace remaining first-party lowercase literals that are not dependency or external URL contracts.
2. Phase 2: backend content cleanup (no path/module renames), with compile checks after each batch.
3. Later phase: dependency/infra/path namespace cutover for `@blockscout/*`, hostnames, and scanner directory/package naming.
